Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Liam Park's escalation and Mina's recovery response using search_emails (query: "Liam Park" or "StormCloud escalation" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Liam Park's escalation email from liam.park@stormcloud.io to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com listing four issues, Jae-won Kim's unsigned Austin lease, missing goodwill credit on their invoice, Simone Richter's stuck stipend reimbursement, and the unanswered Ridgeline Robotics referral; (b) Mina's recovery plan email to Liam (CC chloe.vance@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com) promising concrete fixes on all four items.
OE 2: Search emails for the Jae-won Kim lease thread using search_emails (query: "Jae-won" or "Austin lease" or "guarantor" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest (carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) emailing Suki on April 8 that the landlord at Barton Creek Flats has not countersigned because the corporate guarantor packet is missing; (b) Suki Patel replying she would send the packet by end of week; (c) Jae-won Kim emailing support@moveops.com on April 15 asking for final lease documents; (d) Omar Ibrahim replying with a generic "checking with the relocation team" response. No evidence that the guarantor packet was ever sent to UrbanNest.
OE 3: Search Airtable for Jae-won Kim's relocation record by first using airtable_list_bases then airtable_list_tables to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01) in base appMoveOpsOps001, then using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim"). Expected finding: Record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin, Name: Jae-won Kim, Company: StormCloud Analytics, Status: In Progress, Origin: Seattle WA, Destination: Austin TX, Move Start: 2026-04-07, Move End: 2026-04-09, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements noting "Lease still unsigned, UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork. Record incorrectly shows Completed." The Status field has been corrected to "In Progress" from its earlier incorrect "Completed" state, but the special requirements note still references the prior error.
OE 4: Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Issue linear_issue_a49b4d199139, "StormCloud account incident reconstruction, Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop" (Priority 1, assigned to Mina Hashimoto, created 2026-04-24), this is the master reconstruction ticket with comments from Marcus Thorne confirming the $500 credit posting error and Julian Brooks providing support recovery context; (b) Issue linear_issue_c97359216490, "StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS" (closed prematurely despite unresolved lease); (c) Issue linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents" (Priority 4/Low, unassigned, created from Omar's support triage).
OE 5: Read Linear comments on the reconstruction ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_a49b4d199139"). Expected findings: (a) Marcus Thorne's comment confirming the $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319; (b) Julian Brooks's comment providing the support recovery context, the flight disruption fix was legitimate but credit application was never verified on the billing side.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching StormCloud or INV-2026-002). Expected finding: Invoice inv_stormcloud_01, StormCloud Analytics, DocNumber INV-2026-002, Balance $26,400, 3 line items (2 Denver→Austin at $8,800 each + 1 Denver→Seattle at $8,800). The $500 goodwill credit is NOT reflected on this invoice. The credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287. As of current data, no evidence the credit has been moved to INV-2026-0319, the balance remains $26,400 with no adjustment visible.
OE 7: Search Slack for discussions about the StormCloud credit and stipend using conversations_search_messages (query: "StormCloud credit" or "goodwill" or "EST-2026-0287" or "Simone stipend" or similar). Expected findings: (a) In #finance (C005): Marcus Thorne confirming the $500 goodwill credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287, not the live invoice INV-2026-0319, and stating he will get it moved; (b) In #finance (C005): Mina asking Marcus to release Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement since StormCloud is not part of the flagged fraud cases; (c) In #customer-engagement (C002): David Chen's April 19 celebration of Anya Petrova's Ridgeline referral followed by his April 25 admission that the referral was never turned into a CRM record.
OE 8: Search emails for Simone Richter's stipend reimbursement status using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "standing desk" or "stipend" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Simone Richter (simone.richter@stormcloud.io) emailing Mina on April 16 asking about her $340 standing desk claim submitted March 25 still showing Pending Review; (b) Mina forwarding to Marcus asking to release it. Marcus responded denying the lease, the stipend appears still frozen under the blanket hold.
OE 9: Search emails for any response from Kenji Watanabe after David's and Mina's outreach using search_emails (query: "Kenji" or "Ridgeline" or similar). Expected findings: (a) Anya Petrova's warm introduction email (CC kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com and mina.hashimoto@moveops.com); (b) Kenji's April 21 follow-up (still unread in David's inbox); (c) David's belated reply to Kenji on April 24 offering call times; (d) Mina's separate reply to Kenji on April 24 also offering call times. No response from Kenji after the April 24 outreach from either David or Mina.
OE 10: Search CRM for StormCloud company and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "StormCloud"). Expected findings: Company record company_stormcloud (StormCloud Analytics, Denver CO, SaaS/Analytics, 85 employees, type: Customer). Two deal records, deal_stormcloud (proposal stage, $25,650) and deal_stormcloud_won (closedWon, $26,400 for 3 relocations at $8,800 each).
OE 11: Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics company and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline"). Expected findings: Company crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2 (Ridgeline Robotics, Austin TX, Robotics, 180 employees, type: Prospect, created 2026-04-24). Deal crm_deal_a95b4721982a ($8,750, "Qualified To Buy" stage, described as warm referral from StormCloud Analytics, priority follow-up required).
OE 12: Look up David Chen's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Expected finding: david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 13: Look up the #customer-engagement Slack channel ID using channels_list. Expected finding: Channel C002 = #customer-engagement.
OE 14 [WRITE]: Create an engagement note on the StormCloud CRM record using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: recovery status summary covering all four issues, (1) Jae-won housing: Airtable corrected to In Progress but lease still unsigned, guarantor packet never sent, no evidence of landlord countersignature; (2) Goodwill credit: $500 still on wrong estimate EST-2026-0287, not moved to INV-2026-0319, balance unchanged at $26,400; (3) Simone Richter stipend: $340 standing desk still frozen under blanket hold, no response from finance; (4) Ridgeline referral: outreach sent by David and Mina on April 24, no response from Kenji yet, company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]).
OE 15 [WRITE]: Create an engagement note on the Ridgeline Robotics CRM record using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: status update noting outreach sent by David Chen and Mina Hashimoto on April 24 with proposed call times, no response from Kenji Watanabe as of current date, deal remains at Qualified To Buy, company_ids: ["crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2"]).
OE 16 [WRITE]: Send email to David Chen using send_email (sender: david.chen@moveops.com, recipients: ["david.chen@moveops.com"], subject referencing StormCloud / Ridgeline recovery status or similar, content covering verified status of all four items: (1) Jae-won's lease is still unsigned, Airtable was corrected to In Progress but the guarantor packet was never sent to UrbanNest and the landlord has not countersigned, this remains unresolved; (2) the $500 goodwill credit is still on cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 and has not been moved to active invoice INV-2026-0319, StormCloud's balance is unchanged; (3) Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement is still frozen in pending review with no visible response from Marcus on Mina's release request; (4) Ridgeline Robotics has not responded to either David's or Mina's April 24 outreach, deal is at Qualified To Buy but no engagement since recovery emails were sent. Flag all four items as still unresolved.)
OE 17 [WRITE]: Post a summary in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: summary of StormCloud and Ridgeline recovery status covering the verified state of all four issues from Liam Park's escalation, noting which items are still open so Mina has visibility on what David is tracking).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
